intro: Eight weeks of the trade, kept the way a ledger should be kept — dated, sourced, and in one place. New entries are added at the top each week; the quarter's running numbers live in the Almanac below.

entries.h: Swiss exports snap back 11.2% in June
entries.t: The FH reported June exports of CHF 2.4 billion on 1.3 million pieces (+11.7% volume) — the strongest month of 2026 after April's −16.6% and May's +0.4%. The US took CHF 349M (+12.7%), France doubled to CHF 250M (+103.5%), and the CHF 200–500 band grew 54.1% while CHF 500–3,000 fell 4.7%. H1 stands at CHF 12.8B, −0.7%.

entries.h: Rio Tinto's last carats: Diavik processes its remnants
entries.t: With the Northwest Territories mine closed since March, Q2 yielded 33,000 carats from remaining surface ore, down 97%. H1 output fell 51% to 1.1 million carats; final sales run through end-2026, completing Rio Tinto's exit from diamond mining after Argyle (2020) and Fort à la Corne (2023). Angola exploration continues.

entries.h: Venetia pause enters Section 189A: 1,214 positions
entries.t: The NUM said De Beers' two-year production halt places 1,134 permanent Venetia workers and 80 DBSSSA staff into large-scale retrenchment consultation, demanding reviews of executive costs, retraining, and alternatives to dismissal. The pause was announced July 13 as a cash-preservation measure.

entries.h: US jewelry H1: revenue +8.6% on a 19% higher ticket
entries.t: Tenoris data shows first-half revenue up 8.6% and June up 13%, driven by average purchase prices rising 19–20% while unit sales fell, especially under $1,500. Bracelets led categories at +14%; lab-grown grew double-digit on units while natural diamonds grew single-digit on price.

entries.h: The $4,000 floor survives its third test
entries.t: Spot gold closed Friday 17 July at $4,017.30, up $42.10 on the day, after touching $3,959.30 — within five dollars of the triple bottom at $3,964. Monday opened at $4,018.20 with December hike odds near 82%, Brent at $90.40 and the Fed's July 28–29 meeting ahead. Silver led the complex at $56.76, +1.67%.

entries.h: India's June exports jump 26.5% — the factory restarts
entries.t: Gross gems and jewellery exports reached $2,212.6M in June per GJEPC data: gold jewellery +54.5% to $1,087.7M, cut-and-polished diamonds +8.7% to $846.7M. The quarter stayed flat (+0.04% at $6,612.2M) with polished value −4.1% on volume +8.9% — $681.70 a carat; rough imports −32.4% by value.

entries.h: Botswana keeps all three doors open on the De Beers sale
entries.t: Minister Moeti Mohwasa told parliament on 17 July that the state's 15% carries 'complete freedom': partner with Anglo's preferred bidder — the Gareth Penny-led Global Diamond Consortium, with a Qatari fund and Nir Livnat — pre-empt alone, or bid with a third party. Two bidders remain from six; close eyed by Q4.

entries.h: Gemfields prices the ruby middle market at $66.30
entries.t: The miner's first 'Trade Select' auction — a broader mix of Mozambique ruby qualities plus debut sapphire categories — sold 348,409 of 374,008 carats (93.2%) for $23.1M, 82 of 89 lots. May's higher-quality emerald sale made $26.8M at $146.08/ct with buyers cautious and the rupee ~10% weaker since September.

entries.h: Alrosa certifies 29.8M carats carbon-neutral — for the second year
entries.t: TÜV Austria verified the Russian miner's operations and full 2025 production as carbon-neutral: footprint −1.18 kg CO2e per carat (60% better), facilities absorbing 126,000 tonnes more CO2 than emitted, 76% renewable electricity. G7 import restrictions still bar the goods from Western markets.

entries.h: Gold logs its worst week in six — during a war
entries.t: Spot closed 17 July at $3,985.80, down ~3.4% on the week as a sixth day of strikes on Iran drove oil to one-month highs and CME December hike odds to 73%; Dallas Fed's Logan called for a hike. Silver $55.20; platinum $1,599; palladium $1,244.

entries.h: First-ever delivered F.P. Journe Résonance consigned to Phillips Geneva XXIV
entries.t: No. 022/99R — 38mm platinum, one owner since 1999, serial chosen to match his birth date — estimated CHF 500,000–1M for 7–8 Nov. Phillips' three historic Résonance sales in 2026 total $23M+, incl. Souscription No. 007 at $13.9M.

entries.h: Audemars Piguet UK: sales +29% to £105M, profit down 3%
entries.t: 2025 accounts show £7.8M operating profit on £105M sales; Manchester AP House booked £7.4M sales and a £738K loss in year one; UK property and fittings rose from £2.8M to £17.6M. Clifton Street AP House opened Feb 2026.

entries.h: Bezel rejects 38% of submitted Rolexes at authentication
entries.t: Counterfeits, Frankenwatches, misdescriptions and stolen goods; Omega 13%, Tudor 6%, Cartier 5%, most others 2–3%. Rejected Rolexes are 43% of refused value; Patek 22%. Rolex is 28% of Bezel sales and 34% of wish lists.

entries.h: Brandee Dallow starts as ICA chief executive
entries.t: Effective 6 July; the 1984-founded International Colored Gemstone Association counts ~700 members in 47 countries. Dallow arrives via De Beers, Rio Tinto Diamonds, RJC, Grandview Klein and the Women's Jewelry Association.

entries.h: Anglo names Penny's Global Diamond Consortium preferred bidder for De Beers
entries.t: Announced to Botswana's parliament 17 July by minister Moeti Mohwasa; three shortlisted bidders; consortium plans Angola and Namibia as partners; Botswana (15%) weighs matching rights; close eyed Q4 2026. De Beers carried at $4.9B after $3.5B impairments.

entries.h: India–UK trade pact enters force; jewelry goes duty-free both ways
entries.t: CETA effective 15 July; UK scraps duties on 96.8% of tariff lines (97.7% of trade value); first zero-duty gold, silver and diamond jewelry consignments shipped 16 July. India→UK goods exports $13.44B in FY25–26; 900+ jewelry firms expected to benefit.

entries.h: Rolex CPO premium compresses to 22.6% median as program scales
entries.t: Morgan Stanley × WatchCharts: launch-era ~40% premium now 22.6% median; Q2 program sales $186M; 157 of 712 retailers enrolled; top-three inventory share down from 63% to 48%; median participating retailer's H1 CPO revenue $899k, from $578k.

entries.h: Sotheby's watch sales run 64% ahead at the half; 90% sell-through
entries.t: Latest sale: 1,850 bidders from 60 countries; 45% of lots beat high estimates (double y/y); sales at 200%+ of combined low estimates. Recent prints: Paul Newman 6263 $1.14M, Journe Vagabondage II 'Brady' $1.02M, Brette Artisans Rose $512k.

entries.h: Ekati goes to receivership; Canada's first diamond mine will close
entries.t: B.C. court order 14 July at the NWT government's request; PwC receiver; production stops mid-August after an eight-week sale found no buyer. ~340 workers remain (2024: 700 + 542 contractors); C$326M reclamation security shielded; last sale C$27.4M, C$2M short.

entries.h: Angola's Endiama bids for all of Anglo's 85% of De Beers
entries.t: Confirmed by CEO Ganga Júnior; De Beers carried at $4.9B after $3.5B of impairments, 17 months into Anglo's exit. Botswana (15%) holds the right to match; President Boko calls it a matter of economic sovereignty; ex-De Beers executive groups also bidding.

entries.h: Lab-grown Q2 wholesale: −13% overall, +1% at one carat
entries.t: Edahn Golan's Q2 price list: 2ct rounds −20%, squares −21% (some VVS ranges −25%+), fancies −17%, 1.50–1.99ct −11%; one-carat rounds the only riser at +1%. Rough LGD prices rising — pass-through to polished is the next-quarter question.

entries.h: War week on the metals tape
entries.t: Fifth straight day of US strikes on Iranian sites with the Strait of Hormuz closed: gold fades from a 4,068.90 open to 4,041.10 Thursday (+21.8% YTD); silver 57.07, −16.8% on the month, +53.1% on the year; platinum 1,634.90.

entries.h: June US retail: +0.2% to $768.6B, below forecast
entries.t: Census advance estimate; tax-refund tailwinds faded, online led category gains. Jewelry's H2 setup: a deliberate consumer, gold's 21.8% YTD climb as the value script.

entries.h: Richemont prints a 20 percent quarter
entries.t: Q1 to June 30: group sales €6.3B, +20% constant currency (+17% actual); Americas +27% to €1.7B; direct retail +24%; specialist watchmakers return to growth at +8% to €873M after a -4% FY26.

entries.h: Vietnam's certificate scandal goes global
entries.t: P-Lab ex-director Dang Ngoc Thao among 22 charged over a ring that smuggled 28,000+ diamonds ($10.65M, 141 shipments since 2024) into Vietnam — allegedly swapping GIA inscriptions for P-Lab certificates. PNJ offers free re-certification.

entries.h: An $8.9 billion auction half
entries.t: H1 2026: Christie's $4.5B total (public auctions +71%, 91% sell-through, luxury $539M +15%); Sotheby's $4.4B with a record $826M in private sales (+58%). Younger buyers' average watch spend +60% YoY to $129,000.

entries.h: Cartier leads the pre-owned turn
entries.t: June ChronoPulse: Cartier's secondary-market heat builds 3.5× faster than any tracked brand (+5.9% MoM, ~+10% over six months); 10 of 13 brands up in June, all 13 positive over the half; pre-owned Cartier still ~28.1% under retail.

entries.h: Pomellato's Stile Libero tape
entries.t: 65-piece high jewelry collection: Mandala Chromia necklace with 99 fancy-cut sapphires (~45ct), Drops of Paraíba choker (21 pear Paraíbas, 20.15ct), Ipnotica earrings with 540 diamonds, openwork with gold-lace artisan Sara Bran.

entries.h: Karowe's tenth thousand-carat stone
entries.t: July 13: Lucara recovers a 1,305.4ct white gem diamond, unbroken, via XRT sorting — the tenth 1,000ct-plus stone in the mine's history (Motswedi 2,488ct, Lesedi La Rona 1,109ct). No other mine has produced one.

entries.h: Dubai posts a record $41.7B trade
entries.t: 2025 diamond trade up 16% to $41.7B on 359.5M carats (+43%) — first simultaneous value and volume records; polished +25% to $18.7B; colored gemstones +48% to a record $1.1B.

entries.h: Mumbai takes the WFDB gavel
entries.t: Bharat Diamond Bourse VP Mehul Shah wins the World Federation of Diamond Bourses presidency in a three-way Singapore vote over Dubai's Ahmed Bin Sulayem and Shanghai's Lin Qiang, succeeding Yoram Dvash.

entries.h: WoS is majority-American for the first time
entries.t: FY26 revenue +11% to $2.44B; US sales +18% to $1.24B — over half of group revenue and profit. Guidance: +5–10% for FY27. Results land a day after news of rebuffed takeover approaches.

entries.h: Couture week's monumental tape
entries.t: Boucheron shows a 1,500ct-plus morganite necklace (~3,200 workshop hours); Messika sets the 20.46ct Okavango Blue on a 500-diamond rivière; Chanel debuts 85 pieces; Graff's 12 butterfly brooches convert to pendants.

entries.h: One soft CPI print turns the tape
entries.t: July 14: gold +1.8% to $4,072.60, silver +2.4% to $58.88, platinum +2.4% to $1,636, palladium +4.1% — the whole board rallies on cooler June inflation a day after gold's $3,985.70 intraday stab.

entries.h: Guyana's diggers switch to gold
entries.t: H1 diamond exports fall 70–80% YoY as alluvial miners re-rig for bullion; 85% of Guyana's rough value sits in stones of 0.50ct and under — supply leaving the exact category leading the recovery.

entries.h: Pearls run the table at Bonhams London
entries.t: All top-10 lots beat high estimates July 8; David Morris pearl-and-diamond necklace makes $54,894, a natural pearl strand roughly 4× its estimate, a three-stone ring 3.5×.

entries.h: A 21-year first at the Halstead Grant
entries.t: Navajo silversmith Enoch Platero (Enoch Michael) becomes the first traditional Native American jewelry artist to win the emerging-jeweler award — $7,500 cash plus supplies, judged on a full business plan.

entries.h: Watch liquidity goes instant
entries.t: Arena Club launches mystery 'Time Boxes' (published odds, 90% instant buy-back; Rolex, Patek, AP, Cartier) while The Heritage Club adds AI photo-to-offer valuations with 30–60-day repurchase in the UAE.

entries.h: De Beers cuts the book — and pauses Venetia for two years
entries.t: First sale under the new contract brings official rough prices down from 5–50% above market to near-market levels, sold to a roster cut from ~70 to 45–50 sightholders. Days later De Beers announces a two-year production pause at Venetia — 4,400 jobs at risk, >40% of South Africa's output.

entries.h: Silver's air pocket: −10.9% in a month
entries.t: US–Iran escalation and Fed nerves knock silver to ~$57–58 (from $70+ in late June), platinum to $1,595 (−45% from January's record), and push gold as low as $3,985.70 intraday before it steadies near $4,000.

entries.h: Watches of Switzerland rebuffs takeover interest
entries.t: Reuters: private equity and strategic buyers held talks in recent months; no formal offer. Shares +55% YTD to £7.50 (£1.75B market cap); management wants significantly more. FY26 revenue +11% to £1.8B.

entries.h: Joopiter staffs up like a major house
entries.t: Pharrell Williams' auction platform launches a standalone watch division under Nate Borgelt, ex-head of watches for Bonhams Americas and a specialist in the $24M Graves Supercomplication sale.

entries.h: De Beers opens the July sight ready to realign rough prices
entries.t: The miner arrives in Gaborone prepared to cut official book prices or widen discounted deals — sightholders put the book 20–30% above market in sub-carat rough. A new sightholder contract takes effect against the backdrop of Anglo American's divestment.

entries.h: Gold camps at the $4,100 line
entries.t: Bullion consolidates near the World Gold Council's H2 base case (±5% around $4,100) after June 25's $4,001.80 trough; 30-day volatility cools from 50%+ to under 30%.

entries.h: ICA's new CEO takes office
entries.t: Brandee Dallow (ex-De Beers, Rio Tinto Diamonds, RJC) begins as chief executive of the International Colored Gemstone Association on July 6.

entries.h: Phillips closes a $235M spring — the biggest watch season ever
entries.t: Combined Geneva–Hong Kong–New York sales top $235M at a 99.8% sell-through across 937 lots; an F.P. Journe Souscription Résonance makes $13.9M in New York, a record for any independent watchmaker.

entries.h: US independents post a fewer-better-dearer May
entries.t: Independent jewelers' sales rose 12% YoY on a 22% higher average ticket with 9% fewer units; diamond average prices +21%.

entries.h: Supply discipline turns physical
entries.t: Alrosa suspends Severalmaz mining for ~3 months from July 1; Storm Mountain puts Lesotho's Kao mine on care and maintenance (~750 workers); Petra's Finsch enters business rescue (~1,800 jobs).

entries.h: Gold survives its $4,000 test
entries.t: Spot touches $4,001.80 on June 25 — the H1 low — and holds the level; YTD the metal is down ~7% from a $5,405 January 29 peak yet remains a top-performing asset over 12 months.

entries.h: Russia draws the hardest lab-grown line yet
entries.t: From September 1, lab-grown jewelry tags must read 'synthetic' — the words 'diamond', grading terms, and descriptors like 'natural' or 'eco-friendly' are banned; weights in grams only.

entries.h: Signet steadies, Asia soars
entries.t: Signet Q1: $1.55B (+0.7%), same-store +1.8%, guidance floor raised to $6.7B. Chow Tai Fook FY: revenue +5% to HK$94.4B, profit +52% to a record HK$9.08B; Luk Fook revenue +29%, profit +89%.

entries.h: Swiss exports eke out +0.4% in May
entries.t: CHF 2.11B — US +12%, France +57% (logistics hub), UK +25%, China −21%; CHF 200–500 watches +24% while the CHF 500–3,000 middle falls 17%. YTD −3.1%.

entries.h: New York's blue June
entries.t: Christie's Magnificent Jewels makes $49.7M at 100% sell-through — the 31.62ct Azure Blue at $8.4M. Sotheby's answers with $43.4M at 98%, led by a 10.02ct fancy intense blue at $8.7M.

entries.h: Estate material outperforms — again
entries.t: Christie's Stream Family Collection realizes $17.3M at 99% sold; a 49.91ct light green-yellow diamond quadruples its low estimate at $2M.

entries.h: Rough turns a corner
entries.t: Alrosa logs record May client viewings — double last year, busiest in eight years — with 2–10ct prices up 6–9% since January; TAGS Dubai tender clears 80%.

entries.h: JCK Las Vegas: color claims the counter
entries.t: The State of the Colored Gemstone Industry panel reports engagement customers choosing sapphire, spinel and garnet first; margins beat diamonds; Tucson 2026 ranked among dealers' best shows ever.

entries.h: The three trends of jewelry week
entries.t: Marion Fasel's Vegas read: mechanicals (transformable pieces), creative alternatives to gold (beads, cords, small scale), and narrative jewelry as the feature customers pay for.

entries.h: Pandora widens its lab-grown footprint
entries.t: Lab-grown collections expand to Spain and Italy via Barcelona and Milan flagships — eight markets globally.

entries.h: Who buys diamonds now
entries.t: De Beers' US study: average natural-diamond spend up 25% to $4,063 vs 2023; 75% of value demand is non-bridal; Gen Z takes 23% of value demand vs 18% of population.
entries.s: De Beers via The Diamond Press

entries.h: Jewelry leads a €1.44T luxury market
entries.t: Bain–Altagamma puts 2025 global luxury spending at €1.44T with jewelry the leading category; personal luxury +1% in H1 2026, 2–4% forecast for the year.

entries.h: Geneva rewrites the record book
entries.t: Phillips' Geneva Watch Auction XXIII realizes $96.3M — the highest-grossing watch auction in history — with a Patek Philippe Ref. 2523 at $10.2M and dozens of world records.

entries.h: Richemont's jewelry engine
entries.t: FY2026 (ended March 31): group sales €22.4B (+11% constant); Jewellery Maisons €16.5B (+14%) at a 30.5% operating margin; net profit €3.5B (+27%).

entries.h: London Diamond Bourse elects Charlotte Rose
entries.t: First female president in the bourse's 86-year history — believed the first woman to lead any diamond bourse worldwide.
